Title: R2R3-MYB genes coordinate conical cell development and cuticular wax biosynthesis in Phalaenopsis aphrodite

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing flower buds; in petals transcripts accumulate at Stage 1, peak at Stage 3, and are barely detectable at Stage 5
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In petals, accumulates at Stage 1 and peaks at Stage 3 during conical epidermal cell differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Subgroup 9A R2R3-MYB (MIXTA/MIXTA-like clade). Functions appear conserved for conical cell differentiation and expanded to wax control in this monocot. Putative direct targets were narrowed by combining overexpression/SRDX transcriptomics with temporal expression and WGCNA; PaSHN was not significantly altered and is not supported as downstream target.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticular wax biosynthesis; coordination of epidermal conical cell differentiation with wax deposition
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of wax biosynthetic and cell wall-associated genes; SRDX chimeric repressor suppresses target expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates wax synthesis gene KCS11-like (PAXXG005280) and cell wall-associated genes including microtubule-associated protein TORTIFOLIA1-like (PAXXG201700) and cellulose synthase-like protein D4 (PAXXG003800); SRDX fusion downregulates multiple cell wall-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA637697, PRJNA728444, PRJNA728478; WGCNA; comparative transcriptome analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core regulator that increases petal wax load and is required for proper conical epidermal cell differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for transgenic tobacco; transient overexpression vector used in petals; SRDX chimeric repressor constructs
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Petal developmental stage; transient agroinfiltration; tobacco heterologous overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In transgenic tobacco caused shiny abaxial leaves due to altered wax composition without increased total wax load
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaMYB9A1 is a subgroup 9A R2R3-MYB highly expressed during the period of petal conical cell differentiation. 2. Transient overexpression in Phalaenopsis petals increased total cuticular wax load by about 314% and promoted wax crystal/deposit formation. 3. SRDX repression flattened petal epidermal cells, showing requirement for conical cell differentiation. 4. Comparative transcriptomics identified wax biosynthetic and cell wall-related genes as downstream targets, including KCS11-like. 5. In tobacco, ectopic expression altered wax composition and caused shiny leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering floral cuticular wax deposition and epidermal surface traits in orchids; may be combined with downstream wax-pathway genes, but heterologous expression can alter wax composition in non-target tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cuticular wax serves as a primary barrier against biotic and abiotic stresses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cuticular wax accumulation/composition remodeling in petals and is required for conical epidermal cell differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in petals; SRDX chimeric repression in petals; transgenic tobacco overexpression; SEM; GC-MS wax profiling; RNA-seq comparative transcriptomics; RT-qPCR

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing flower buds; predominantly expressed at petal Stages 3 and 4
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in petals at Stages 3 and 4 during conical epidermal cell differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Subgroup 9A R2R3-MYB (MIXTA/MIXTA-like clade). Coordinates conical cell differentiation and wax biosynthesis in monocot petals. PaSHN was tested and not supported as a downstream target.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticular wax biosynthesis; coordination of epidermal conical cell differentiation with wax deposition
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of wax biosynthetic and cell wall-associated genes; SRDX chimeric repressor suppresses target expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates fatty alcohol synthesis gene FAR (PAXXG145540); SRDX fusion downregulates multiple cell wall-associated genes including PAN1-like, cellulose synthase-like, xyloglucan endotransglucosylase/hydrolase, and pectinesterase-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; BioProject PRJNA637697, PRJNA728444, PRJNA728478; WGCNA; comparative transcriptome analysis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core regulator that increases petal wax load and is required for proper conical epidermal cell differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for transgenic tobacco; transient overexpression vector used in petals; SRDX chimeric repressor constructs
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Petal developmental stage; transient agroinfiltration; tobacco heterologous overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In transgenic tobacco caused shiny abaxial leaves due to altered wax composition without increased total wax load
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaMYB9A2 is a subgroup 9A R2R3-MYB expressed during the key stages of petal conical cell differentiation. 2. Transient overexpression in Phalaenopsis petals increased total cuticular wax load by about 212% and promoted wax crystal/deposit formation. 3. SRDX repression flattened petal epidermal cells, showing requirement for conical cell differentiation. 4. Comparative transcriptomics identified a wax biosynthetic target, FAR (PAXXG145540), and many cell wall-related downstream genes. 5. In tobacco, ectopic expression altered wax composition and caused shiny leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering floral wax and epidermal traits in orchids; can be paired with fatty alcohol pathway engineering, but ectopic expression in vegetative tissues may alter leaf surface properties.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAXXG145540 (FAR); PAXXG325960 (PAN1-like); PAXXG003800 (cellulose synthase-like protein D4); multiple XTH and pectinesterase genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cuticular wax serves as a primary barrier against biotic and abiotic stresses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cuticular wax accumulation/composition remodeling in petals and is required for conical epidermal cell differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in petals; SRDX chimeric repression in petals; transgenic tobacco overexpression; SEM; GC-MS wax profiling; RNA-seq comparative transcriptomics; RT-qPCR
